title: Nissan Jatco CVT P17F0 Diagnosis and Line Pressure Recovery
description: P17F0 (CVT line pressure low) is the most common Nissan Jatco CVT fault code, especially after fluid service or on high-mileage units. Covers diagnosis, fluid pressure recovery procedure, and when to recommend replacement.
common_symptoms: P17F0 CVT fluid line pressure low
belt slip felt as sudden RPM flare without speed increase
physical_solution: Verify correct NS-3 fluid and level. Perform line pressure recovery procedure. Check for internal leaks at side cover gasket. If pressure unrecoverable: CVT replacement or remanufactured unit required.

uds_services: $22 ReadDataByIdentifier (CVT line pressure, belt ratio, step motor position)
technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Partial — P17F0 readable via Mode $03 or $19 to TCM. CVT line pressure DID via $22 to TCM 0x7E1 with CONSULT emulation addressing. ELM327 $31 for adaptation reset accessible on 2012-2016 models. CONSULT III+ preferred for full CVT data.
preconditions: Verify NS-3 fluid ONLY installed (never Dexron/generic — instant further damage)
Fluid level checked at operating temperature (35-45°C)
No physical damage to CVT (no metallic debris in fluid)

procedure_steps.description: STOP: Verify fluid type first. If generic CVT fluid was used during service, the Jatco belt coating is already compromised. NS-3 only. If wrong fluid used: drain completely, refill NS-3, but prognosis for full recovery is poor — advise customer.

Read CVT line pressure: $22 [CVT_LINE_PRESS_DID] to TCM 0x7E1. Target at idle: 350-450 kPa. Target at stall: 900-1100 kPa. P17F0 sets when measured pressure < 300 kPa. Near zero pressure = pump failure or severe internal leak.

Read belt ratio and step motor position: $22 [BELT_RATIO_DID] and $22 [STEP_MOTOR_DID]. If ratio stuck (step motor not moving): step motor failure separate from pressure issue. Belt ratio should vary smoothly 0.4 to 2.4 across driving range.

LINE PRESSURE RECOVERY ATTEMPT (mild cases): Perform complete NS-3 fluid exchange (not just drain — use flush machine or multi-drain). 8-10 quarts total exchange on JF011E. Fresh NS-3 often restores 100-150 kPa of pressure in mild cases.
Adaptation reset after fluid exchange: $31 01 [CVT_ADAPT_ROUTINE] to TCM. Clears learned belt clamp pressures so TCM recalibrates with fresh fluid.

DRIVE CYCLE AFTER RESET: 10 minutes gentle driving. No WOT. Allow CVT to cycle through full ratio range at light throttle. TCM relearns pressure requirements. Monitor: no sudden RPM flare, smooth acceleration.

REPLACEMENT CRITERIA: P17F0 persists after complete fluid exchange + reset = internal pump wear, side cover leak, or belt/pulley wear. Options: (1) Jatco remanufactured exchange unit, (2) transmission specialist rebuild. CVT repair cost typically $3,500-$5,000. Set expectation with customer early.
After successful recovery: P17F0 cleared, line pressure at idle 400+ kPa, smooth acceleration, no RPM flare. Monitor for 30-day recurrence. If P17F0 returns within 30 days: CVT replacement required.

oem_notes.Nissan/Infiniti: JF011E (RE0F10A): Altima/Sentra/Rogue 2002-2018. Most common failure. Capacity: 8.7 quarts total. JF016E (RE0F10D): Rogue/Murano 2014+. Capacity: 9.3 quarts. NS-3 fluid mandatory — part number Nissan 999MP-NS300P. Jatco CVT extended warranty: Nissan extended CVT coverage to 10 years/120k miles on many 2012-2017 models with P17F0 — ALWAYS check warranty status before recommending customer pay for repair. Nissan TSB NTB16-076: CVT control valve body replacement for persistent P17F0 on 2015-2018 Altima.
